citations.title: Simulator baseline dataset in this repository
citations.note: Primary Year-0 model state loaded directly by the app.

assumptions.text: GDP is aggregated as C + I + G(goods/services) + NX each simulated year.
Household consumption is max(survival_cost, disposable_income * MPC); VAT is computed from gross consumption.
Firms invest 20% of revenue scaled by (1 + 5% growth expectation); corporate tax is 15% of revenue times CIT rate.
Unemployment and inflation adjust using simple threshold rules based on GDP growth; poverty rises when low-income disposable income falls below survival cost.
Policy sliders start from statutory baselines: VAT 12%, CIT 25%, PIT schedule from TRAIN.
Income-share and high-income household concentration are calibrated as a proxy layer informed by poverty incidence and inequality indicators.
